headline: Ukraine Rewilding Thousands of Hectares Amid Conflict
meta_description: Despite invasion and conflict, Ukraine has restored over 13,500 hectares of wetlands and grasslands, bringing wild horses, buffalo, and deer back home.
summary_short: Amid Russia's invasion, Ukraine continues rewilding more than 13,500 hectares, restoring habitats for wild horses, buffalo, deer, and kulan.
body:
Conflict and conservation might seem incompatible, yet Ukraine has continued one of its most ambitious ecological restoration projects even during invasion. Over 13,500 hectares of wetlands and grasslands have been rewilded, bringing large herbivores back to landscapes that had lost them. Wild horses, buffalo, deer, and kulan now roam these restored territories.

This rewilding effort is not simply about wildlife—it is about ecosystem function. Large herbivores are keystone species; their grazing, movement, and presence shape entire landscapes, affecting vegetation patterns, soil health, and water cycles. The restoration of these species helps rebuild ecosystems that had been degraded or simplified by human activity.

That Ukraine has maintained this commitment to ecological restoration during such difficult circumstances speaks to the deep value placed on these efforts. The restored landscapes will benefit both wildlife and human communities, providing habitat resilience and supporting the natural systems that communities depend on. This work represents a form of hope—an investment in the land's future even amid present hardship.
